content.problemStatement:
There is an N├ЧNN\times NN├ЧN grid, with rows numbered 111 to NNN from top to bottom and columns numbered 111 to NNN from left to right. The cell at the intersection of the iii-th row and jjj-th column is denoted (i,j)(i, j)(i,j).

Chef is standing at position (x1,y1)(x_1, y_1)(x1тАЛ,y1тАЛ) of this grid. He wants to reach position (x2,y2)(x_2, y_2)(x2тАЛ,y2тАЛ).

To achieve this, he can take either horizontal or vertical steps.
That is, from cell (x,y)(x, y)(x,y), he can move to one of (xтИТ1,y),(x+1,y),(x,yтИТ1),(x,y+1)(x-1, y), (x+1, y), (x, y-1), (x, y+1)(xтИТ1,y),(x+1,y),(x,yтИТ1),(x,y+1).

However, Chef **does not need to stay within the grid**, that is, he can choose to go outside it if he so wishes.

The cost of a horizontal/vertical step is 000 if **both** the starting and ending square lie outside the grid, and 111 otherwise.

Formally, suppose Chef takes one step from (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) to (xтА▓,yтА▓)(x', y')(xтА▓,yтА▓). Then,


If (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) lies within the grid, the cost of this step is 111
If (xтА▓,yтА▓)(x', y')(xтА▓,yтА▓) lies within the grid, the cost of this step is 111
If both the above don't hold, the cost of this step is 000
Find the **minimum cost** to reach (x2,y2)(x_2, y_2)(x2тАЛ,y2тАЛ) from (x1,y1)(x_1, y_1)(x1тАЛ,y1тАЛ).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of
test cases.
Each test case consists of one line of input containing five space-separated integers NNN, x1x_1x1тАЛ, y1y_1y1тАЛ, x2x_2x2тАЛ, and y2y_2y2тАЛ.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum cost of traveling from (x1,y1)(x_1, y_1)(x1тАЛ,y1тАЛ) to (x2,y2)(x_2, y_2)(x2тАЛ,y2тАЛ).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: One optimal sequence of moves is as follows:

Chef moves from (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1) to (0,1)(0, 1)(0,1) with a cost of 111. Note that (0,1)(0, 1)(0,1) is outside the grid.
Now, Chef can move on the outside from (0,1)(0, 1)(0,1) to (2,4)(2, 4)(2,4) with no cost.
Finally, Chef moves from (2,4)(2, 4)(2,4) to (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3) with cost 111, for a total cost of 222.

Test case 222: Chef can move as follows: (2,3)тЖТ(3,3)тЖТ(3,4)(2, 3) \to (3, 3) \to (3, 4)(2,3)тЖТ(3,3)тЖТ(3,4), for a cost of 222.
Test case 444: Chef can move as follows: (2,1)тЖТ(2,0)(2, 1) \to (2, 0)(2,1)тЖТ(2,0) with cost 111, then (2,0)тЖТ(5,7)(2, 0) \to (5, 7)(2,0)тЖТ(5,7) on the outside with cost 000, then (5,7)тЖТ(5,6)тЖТ(5,5)(5, 7) \to (5, 6) \to (5, 5)(5,7)тЖТ(5,6)тЖТ(5,5) with cost 222 for a total cost of 333.
